title: May Day Magic and Flower Power
story: Dancing around tall poles with colorful ribbons streaming behind you sounds like a fairy tale, but it's exactly what happens on May 1 during May Day celebrations! People weave maypoles with bright ribbons, give bouquets of fresh flowers to neighbors, and crown someone as the May Queen. Some communities hold parades filled with flower decorations and folk dancing that makes everyone smile.
activity_question: What do people dance around during May Day celebrations?
follow_up_question: What flowers would you pick to make the perfect May Day bouquet?

title: Workers Unite on Labour Day!
story: Workers around the world celebrate Labour Day on May 1 by taking a well-deserved break from their jobs! This special holiday honors all the hardworking people who build our cities, teach our classes, fix our cars, and keep our world running smoothly. Many countries hold parades where workers march proudly through the streets, carrying colorful banners and signs. Families enjoy picnics and festivals together on this day off.
activity_question: What do many countries do to celebrate Labour Day?
bonus_fact: Over 80 countries celebrate Labour Day on May 1st!

title: Paws-itively Perfect Purebred Pups!
story: Dog lovers everywhere celebrate their favorite purebred pups on May 1 during National Purebred Dog Day! People share photos of their Golden Retrievers, German Shepherds, Poodles, and hundreds of other purebred breeds on social media. Dog shows and breed clubs host special events to showcase what makes each breed unique. Owners dress up their dogs, visit dog parks for breed meetups, and some even bake special dog treats shaped like bones. It's a day to appreciate the amazing variety of purebred dogs and celebrate responsible breeding that keeps these special bloodlines healthy and strong.
activity_question: What do people commonly share on social media during National Purebred Dog Day?
bonus_fact: There are over 340 recognized dog breeds worldwide!

title: Mother Goose Gets Her Day!
story: Nursery rhymes take center stage every May 1 on National Mother Goose Day! Kids and grown-ups celebrate by reading classic tales like "Humpty Dumpty" and "Jack and Jill" together. Libraries host special story times, teachers organize rhyming games, and families dust off old picture books filled with these timeless characters. Many people share their favorite Mother Goose memories on social media, posting photos of beloved childhood books. It's the perfect day to remember how these simple, silly poems helped us learn to read and sparked our love for stories that never get old.
activity_question: What do libraries often do to celebrate National Mother Goose Day?
follow_up_question: Which Mother Goose rhyme do you think teaches the best lesson?

title: Parfait-ly Sweet Chocolate Day!
story: Every May 1, chocolate lovers celebrate National Chocolate Parfait Day by creating towering treats in tall glasses! A parfait is like a delicious edible tower made with layers of chocolate pudding, whipped cream, cake crumbs, and sometimes fresh berries or nuts. People love posting photos of their colorful creations on social media, showing off their artistic layering skills. Ice cream shops and restaurants often feature special chocolate parfait deals, while families gather in kitchens to build their own sweet masterpieces. The best part? You get to eat your artwork when you're done!
activity_question: What makes a parfait look like a tower?
activity_choices: It's served in tall glasses with layers
activity_answer: It's served in tall glasses with layers
bonus_fact: The word 'parfait' comes from French and means 'perfect'!
